beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Our framework, termed Vision ARC (VARC), achieves 60.4% accuracy on the ARC-1 benchmark, substantially outperforming existing methods.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Abstraction and inference can arise directly from visual learning, without explicit linguistic intermediates.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: VARC achieves 54.5% accuracy on the ARC-1 benchmark, using a small model... substantially surpasses the best recurrent methods.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Combining VARC models through ensembling... improves accuracy to 60.4%, matching the reported average human performance.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Our VARC with 18M parameters is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, a >20% relative improvement.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: our model performs fully feedforward inference, with no recurrence involved in reasoning.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: VARC correctly solves these challenging tasks.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: we perform test-time training... to adapt the model to the task, enabling it to generalize from only a few examples.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: the prediction from Xinfer becomes progressively more accurate, with the model finally generating the correct prediction.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: all U-Net variants achieve decent accuracy, suggesting that this problem can also be effectively addressed by classical vision backbones.

beliefs.gloss: patchification/2D pos embed incorporate visual inductive biases
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial.

beliefs.gloss: patchification/2D pos embed incorporate visual inductive biases
beliefs.evidence: we replace the 2D ROPE... with a 1D ROPE and observe a degradation of 3.5 points.

beliefs.gloss: canvas patchification enriches data, reduces overfitting, learns spatial priors
beliefs.evidence: each patch can cover multiple colors... which substantially enriches the data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: patchification/2D pos embed incorporate visual inductive biases
beliefs.evidence: A key design principle of our method is to prepare the input as a natural image. This enables the expansion of the token set.

beliefs.gloss: canvas patchification enriches data, reduces overfitting, learns spatial priors
beliefs.evidence: This formulation is important for improving generalization performance.

beliefs.gloss: patchification/2D pos embed incorporate visual inductive biases
beliefs.evidence: Like convolution, it incorporates several critical inductive biases in vision: most notably, locality... and translation invariance.

beliefs.gloss: canvas/augmentations introduce translation/scale invariance for generalization
beliefs.evidence: fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas... yields an additional gain of 2.9 points.

beliefs.gloss: canvas/augmentations introduce translation/scale invariance for generalization
beliefs.evidence: Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain of 6.2 points.

beliefs.gloss: patchification/2D pos embed incorporate visual inductive biases
beliefs.evidence: This can explain why scale augmen- tation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: canvas/augmentations introduce translation/scale invariance for generalization
beliefs.evidence: These data augmentations encourage the model to learn underlying mappings invariant to geometric transformations grounded in the visual world.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: increasing depth and/or width leads to higher accuracy as a result of better fitting.

beliefs.gloss: visual common sense learned from training data
beliefs.evidence: offline training greatly improves the per- formance of TTT, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: even without offline training, our TTT strategy can achieve nontrivial accuracy... suggesting that some tasks... can be solved tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: performing TTT independently for each test task yields substantially better performance (by ~10 points) than doing so jointly.

beliefs.gloss: larger models in current setting lead to overfitting
beliefs.evidence: We hypothesize that overtraining on the test tasks may cause the model to forget the knowledge acquired during offline training.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Single-view inference has a decent pass@1 accuracy of 35.9.

beliefs.gloss: majority voting consolidates multi-view predictions for accuracy
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: majority voting consolidates multi-view predictions for accuracy
beliefs.evidence: To support this metric, we adopt majority voting in multi-view inference and retain the top-2 most populated output solutions.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: To support this metric, we adopt majority voting in multi-view inference and retain the top-2 most populated output solutions.

beliefs.gloss: single pixel error makes ARC prediction incorrect
beliefs.evidence: in ARC, a mistake on even a single pixel renders the entire prediction incorrect.

beliefs.gloss: single pixel error makes ARC prediction incorrect
beliefs.evidence: This may explain the large gain seen here.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Our VARC with 18M parameters is ~10 points better than TRM on ARC-1, a >20% relative improvement.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Our model compares favorably with some of the most powerful LLMs... Our method does not rely on such data and uses a model that is several orders of magnitude smaller.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Our model compares favorably with some of the most powerful LLMs... Our method does not rely on such data and uses a model that is several orders of magnitude smaller.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: our method substantially outperforms the recurrent models: HRM [53] and TRM [27].

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: our method substantially outperforms the recurrent models: HRM [53] and TRM [27].

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Doing so boosts our result to 60.4.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: This result closes the gap with the re- ported average human performance (60.2 [31]).

beliefs.gloss: model reasons by attending to source-target pixel relationships
beliefs.evidence: These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel.

beliefs.gloss: Transformer layers focus on different structures by depth
beliefs.evidence: Different layers exhibit different behavior.

beliefs.gloss: Transformer layers focus on different structures by depth
beliefs.evidence: different layers exhibit different specialties: some layers at- tend to the pixels that are to be copied, and some layers attend to the target lines.

beliefs.gloss: visual common sense learned from training data
beliefs.evidence: increasing the amount of offline training data is beneficial, although the returns diminish beyond a certain point.

beliefs.gloss: visual common sense learned from training data
beliefs.evidence: the diversity of training tasks is helpful for generalization.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: Using only the original ARC training data, without any RE-ARC data, our method achieves a decent accuracy of 31.5.

beliefs.gloss: visual common sense learned from training data
beliefs.evidence: increasing the amount of offline training data is beneficial, although the returns diminish beyond a certain point.

beliefs.gloss: visual common sense learned from training data
beliefs.evidence: the diversity of training tasks is helpful for generalization.

beliefs.gloss: majority voting consolidates multi-view predictions for accuracy
beliefs.evidence: As expected, as the number of proposals (k) increases, the pass@k accuracy increases.

beliefs.gloss: majority voting consolidates multi-view predictions for accuracy
beliefs.evidence: This result indicates that our model produces correct predictions in some of the many views.

beliefs.gloss: most ARC tasks unambiguous, some have multiple plausible rules
beliefs.evidence: our method uncovers different solutions that are plausible.

beliefs.gloss: most ARC tasks unambiguous, some have multiple plausible rules
beliefs.evidence: the rule can be interpreted as either "turn the red box blue only if the extended blue lines go through the box" (our method's first guess).

beliefs.gloss: most ARC tasks unambiguous, some have multiple plausible rules
beliefs.evidence: the rule can be interpreted as... "turn the red box blue if the extended blue lines touch the box in any form" (our method's second guess).

beliefs.gloss: Transformer layers focus on different structures by depth
beliefs.evidence: Layers at different depths tend to focus on different structures. Early layers tend to focus on local transformations and context. Middle layers tend to perform a more non-local connection... The deep layers are more task-specialized.

beliefs.gloss: Transformer layers focus on different structures by depth
beliefs.evidence: different layers exhibit different specialties: some layers at- tend to the pixels that are to be copied, and some layers attend to the target lines.

beliefs.gloss: abstraction/inference from visual learning without language
beliefs.evidence: As the test-time training progresses, the model's predictions gradually converge toward the correct output.
